/readme.txt.exercises: single-cluster read path + lowercase case-flag rendering

/big.bin.exercises: multi-cluster FAT table following + EOC sentinel handling

/Long Filename Example.txt.exercises: LFN chain assembly + short_name_checksum spec conformance

deferred_follow_ups: FAT12 variant (requires larger-cluster or small-size image — synth unit tests cover the packed-entry decode path)
FAT32 variant (requires ≥33 MiB image — synth unit tests cover the root-cluster-as-chain path)
exFAT walker (out of v0.15.0 scope per SPRINTS_v15 deferral clause)
Date/time decoding in VfsEntry.created/modified/accessed (Phase B Part 3 equivalent to HFS+)
notes: newfs_msdos on macOS writes a full LFN chain for names that don't fit 8.3 case-insensitively (like 'Long Filename Example.txt'), and uses the NT case-preservation flag byte at directory entry offset 12 for names that fit 8.3 in lowercase (like 'readme.txt', 'big.bin', 'dir1'). The walker must honor both mechanisms — failing to honor the case-flag byte surfaces 'README.TXT' instead of 'readme.txt' and breaks test assertions that match the user's original writes.
Case-flag bits: 0x08 = name lowercase, 0x10 = extension lowercase. Documented in the FAT long filename extension spec; used by both Windows NT and macOS FAT drivers.

FAT16 is chosen over FAT32 for committable fixture size. FAT32's 65525-cluster minimum forces ≥33 MiB images, which would bloat the repo. FAT12 + FAT32 code paths get synth unit-test coverage in fat_walker/mod.rs.
